examples: has some strange ideas
“I thought he'd help.” “What gave you that idea?”
a child's idea of time
He has a clear idea of his responsibilities.
I have no idea what you're talking about.
The idea of the game is to get the lowest score.
I get the idea. =I understand
Hey! What's the big idea? =why are you doing that?
Leaving early is a good idea.
Don't put ideas into his head. =make him think about doing something he shouldn't
A quiet night at home is my idea of a good time.
I can't remember anything. I remember only ideas and sensations.

enrichment.etymology: Middle English, from Latin, from Greek, from idein to see
